article_title: etcd Ships Final v3.4 Patch, Ending Support for a Release That Has Anchored Kubernetes Since 2019
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: etcd Ships Final v3.4 Patch, Ending Support for a Release That Has Anchored Kubernetes Since 2019
content_preview.summary: SIG-etcd released v3.4.45 alongside v3.5.31 and v3.6.12, marking the end of support for the v3.4 series and pushing remaining users to upgrade.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

SIG-etcd has shipped the final patch for the etcd v3.4 series, closing out a release line that has underpinned Kubernetes clusters since 2019. According to the [etcd project](https://etcd.io/blog/2026/june-patch-release/), the June 1, 2026 update delivered v3.4.45 alongside v3.5.31 and v3.6.12, and explicitly marks the end of support for v3.4. The [v3.4.45 tag](https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/releases/tag/v3.4.45) was published the same day on the project's GitHub repository.

etcd ...

Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News piece (610 words, within the 400-1200 range). Standard Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis scaffolding. Technical depth is appropriate and the lifecycle framing is accurate and restrained. Headline and summary are precise and non-sensational.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots read from disk (no archive fallback; all HTTP 200). source-0.html.gz (etcd.io/blog/2026/june-patch-release): confirms June 1 2026 release of v3.4.45 / v3.5.31 / v3.6.12 and EOL of v3.4. Verbatim quotes verified: 'This update marks the end of support (EOL) for v3.4, originally released in August 2019. No further patches will be issued by the Kubernetes project.', 'should begin the upgrade process as soon as possible', 'should update at the next scheduled maintenance window', 'If you are still using v3.4, please upgrade to a supported version as soon as you can', and 'which patches multiple security vulnerabilities in go' (source attributes the bump to 'golang v1.25.10', matching the article's 'Go 1.25.10'). source-1.html.gz (github.com/etcd-io/etcd release tag): title 'Release v3.4.45 - etcd-io/etcd', published datetime 2026-06-01 — corroborates the load-bearing v3.4.45 version and June 1 date from an allowlisted domain. source-2.html.gz (kubernetes.io announcing-etcd-3.6): verbatim 'the first minor release since etcd v3.5.0 on June 15, 2021' and 'etcd has joined Kubernetes as a SIG (sig-etcd), enabling us to improve project sustainability'. source-3.html.gz (kubernetes.io components doc): verbatim 'Consistent and highly-available key value store for all API server data'. Internal cross-reference to /article/2026-05/21-etcd-370-beta-arrives-... resolves to an existing published article.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (v3.4.45, v3.5.31, v3.6.12, June 1 2026, August 2019, Go/golang v1.25.10, etcd v3.5.0 on June 15 2021) traces verbatim to a cited source. No hallucinated quotes or fabricated specifics detected. The load-bearing headline fact (final v3.4 patch / EOL) is corroborated across both the etcd.io blog and the allowlisted GitHub release tag, so it does not rest solely on the off-allowlist source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No editorializing or AI self-reference. Operator guidance is reported, not advocated.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. Only prior etcd coverage is the 3.7.0-beta article (2026-05/21), which is correctly cited as a cross-reference rather than duplicated. No overlap with other June 2026 articles.
concerns: One source, etcd.io (https://etcd.io/blog/2026/june-patch-release/), is not in config/source_allowlist.txt. This is a non-blocking warning: etcd.io is the official CNCF etcd project domain and the sole authoritative host for this announcement. The headline and lead are corroborated by the allowlisted GitHub release tag (v3.4.45, 2026-06-01) and kubernetes.io, so the article does not depend solely on the off-allowlist source. Documented in the corrections record for reader transparency; the allowlist was not modified as part of this review.
recommendations: Maintainer may consider adding etcd.io to config/source_allowlist.txt given it is the canonical primary source for etcd project announcements, but this is out of scope for the review and left to the maintainer.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. The article is accurate, neutral, and well-sourced; every quote and specific is verbatim from a cited source, and the central fact is corroborated by an allowlisted domain. The sole issue is the off-allowlist etcd.io primary source, which is honestly documentable in a corrections note. Publishing with a corrections trail is the appropriate outcome.
